ogTitle: McMahons Point vs Meadow Flat: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McMahons Point and Meadow Flat.
heading: McMahons Point vs Meadow Flat — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McMahons Point, New South Wales and Meadow Flat, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Meadow Flat has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to McMahons Point (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McMahons Point or Meadow Flat safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McMahons Point has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Meadow Flat scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McMahons Point scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Meadow Flat scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McMahons Point or Meadow Flat?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Meadow Flat has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
